commands.label: Refresh View

examples: refreshview to latest label in rnerolu_v_bug_39435490_bronze
parameter_hints: Extract exactly one ADE view name from the request or current resumed issue context.
If the view is missing or ambiguous, ask only for the view with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
After the requester has approved refreshview and the view is known, do not run refreshview inside Codex; emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.refresh-view view=<viewname>.

approval.guidance: Refreshview is a state-changing ADE lifecycle command. Confirm the target view first; after approval, hand off to the parent runner with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.refresh-view view=<viewname>.

instructions: ADE refreshview execution must happen outside the Codex child process. Codex should interpret natural-language requests, verify the target view and approval, then emit a final standalone handoff line: CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.refresh-view view=<viewname>.
The parent runner executes this command through the repo-common scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper with the default lightweight FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC=TRUE path.
Use at least the standard ADE lifecycle timeout budget; refreshview commonly needs up to 10 minutes.
If refreshview fails with ade_info.vmd.free or another ADE lock, follow ADE persist lock guidance and do not remove ade_locks.wrd automatically.

runner_execution.summary: Run ADE refreshview outside Codex through the local RunInView wrapper.

commands.label: Clean View

parameter_hints: Extract exactly one ADE view name from the request or current resumed issue context.
If the view is missing or ambiguous, ask only for the view with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
After the requester has approved cleanview and the view is known, do not run cleanview inside Codex; emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.clean-view view=<viewname>.

approval.guidance: Cleanview is a state-changing ADE lifecycle command. Confirm the target view first; after approval, hand off to the parent runner with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.clean-view view=<viewname>.

instructions: ADE cleanview execution must happen outside the Codex child process. Codex should interpret natural-language requests, verify the target view and approval, then emit a final standalone handoff line: CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: ade.clean-view view=<viewname>.
The parent runner executes this command through the repo-common scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper with the default lightweight FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC=TRUE path.
Cleanview must be its own parent-runner command. Do not chain commands after cleanview in the same ADE session.
If PMC should run after cleanview, emit a separate later handoff line for CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: hcm.pmc bug=<bugid> view=<viewname>; the PMC parent command re-enters with --full-fmwc.

runner_execution.summary: Run ADE cleanview outside Codex through the local RunInView wrapper.

commands.label: Destroy ADE View

examples: verify the view has no open transaction then destroy view with force option
parameter_hints: Use the current issue or resumed session context to infer exactly one ADE view when the follow-up does not repeat it.
If no unique view can be identified, ask only for the view with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
Proceed only when the requester explicitly asked to destroy, remove, delete, or cleanup the view.

instructions: ADE view cleanup: the requested action is view cleanup, not MergeReq status readback.
Before destroyview, verify the view has no open transaction and no checked-out files with ADE commands.
Use `ade destroyview -force -aborttrans <view>` only for the explicitly requested view.
Do not manually remove view directories or ADE lock files.

commands.label: Create OraReview
instructions: Use the orareview skill for upload/update workflow and required preflight checks.

For a first OraReview upload, no existing review is expected. If orareview -x or orareview -t <txn> -x says no review exists, and the approved task is to create OraReview after verifying the ADE view, transaction, saved changes, and reviewers, continue with CreateOra.sh and verify status after creation.
Use OraReview MCP tools for read-only review metadata, reviewer status, comments, and merge-check details when helpful.

parameter_hints: Extract exactly one bug number or rnerolu_bug-<number> reference from the request.

HCM bug validation does not need an ADE view. Once the bug reference is known and execution is approved, do not run hcmbugvalidation inside Codex; emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: hcm.bug-validation bug_ref=<bugid> so the parent runner executes it outside the child sandbox.
If the request has no bug id or more than one plausible bug id, ask for clarification with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.

postcondition.description: The PASS verdict must be a standalone word in the validation output.
postcondition.failure_guidance: Do not run subsequent commands. Summarize the useful validation output and wait for user verification or follow-up fix instructions.

followup_remediation.summary: Use BugDB MCP for explicit BugDB updates needed to fix validation when available; if MCP lacks field-update support or cannot resolve FusionApps tracking groups, use dev-bugdb-helper for approved field and transaction updates, then rerun the same HCM bug validation command.
instructions: Prefer BugDB MCP for existing bug updates when the needed field-update tool exists.

If MCP cannot update existing bug fields, use $CODEX_HOME/skills/dev-bugdb-helper/scripts/run-bugdb.sh for approved field updates only; CODEX_HOME should be /scratch/<runner-user>/.codex, the wrapper is pinned to /bin/python3.11, and global python3 must not be changed.

Resolve friendly field labels such as Bug Fix ETA, Bug Fix By, or Component Version to exact BugDB API keys before constructing a bug-update payload. For product_id 2419, use cached metadata at docs/bugdb_header_fields_2419.json; call header-fields only if the cache is missing or does not contain the requested label. Do not guess field keys.
Do not print OAuth tokens or client secrets.
For product_id 2419, Fix ETA maps to fixeta; write it in DD-MON-YYYY format such as 12-JUN-2026 because ISO date strings can fail with ORA-01861 even though readback displays ISO format.
Bug Type (FusionApps) is a tracking group, not a bug header field, and dev-bugdb-helper bugsmart-closure only posts the closure template comment; it does not update tracking group data.

For a normal HCM defect/code-fix bug, set Bug Type (FusionApps) to Defect with dev-bugdb-helper bug-update-transactions, preserving required header fields and including reassign_bug=N plus tg=[{tg_id:2963,tg_operation:C,tg_new_value:Defect}]. Use Tracking, Fwd Port, or Backport only when the workflow explicitly calls for that type.
If BugDB MCP add_tracking_group_data_to_bug returns Unknown tracking group name for Bug Type (FusionApps), use the bug-update-transactions path instead of asking for manual input.
Verify BugDB audit/readback after updates, including CHG: Fix ETA->... and CHG: Bug Type (FusionApps)->Defect when applicable.
After applying explicit BugDB follow-up instructions, rerun validation and keep gating until exit code 0 plus standalone PASS.

runner_execution.summary: Run HCM bug validation outside Codex directly from the parent runner environment; no ADE view entry is required.

commands.label: Run HCM bug validation outside Codex
HCM PMC

parameter_hints: Extract exactly one numeric bug id, accepting either a bare number or rnerolu_bug-<number> reference.
Derive the transaction name as rnerolu_bug-<bugid> and use that transaction to identify the target ADE view.

Natural-language PMC requests such as run PMC for bug <bugid> are handled by Codex first; after the bug, view, and approval are clear, emit CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: hcm.pmc bug=<bugid> view=<viewname> so the parent runner executes the reusable PMC command outside Codex.
If no bug id, multiple bug ids, or multiple matching view/transaction candidates are found, ask for clarification with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.

preflight: Extract exactly one numeric bug id and derive transaction name rnerolu_bug-{{bug}}.
Identify the ADE view whose active or target transaction is rnerolu_bug-{{bug}} using ADE as the source of truth; if already inside a view, verify it with ade pwv first.
If no unique matching view and transaction can be identified, stop and ask the requester for the view or transaction choice with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
Before execution, summarize the identified view, transaction, and exact PMC command sequence that will run outside Codex.

approval.guidance: Codex should interpret natural-language PMC requests, confirm the target bug/view when needed, and hand off to the parent runner with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: hcm.pmc bug=<bugid> view=<viewname>. If either value is missing or ambiguous, ask only for that value. Do not run pmc_wrapper from inside the Codex session.

instructions: Run only inside the confirmed ADE view with active transaction rnerolu_bug-{{bug}}.

PMC execution must happen outside the Codex child process. For explicit catalog Commands blocks and Codex CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND handoff lines, the parent runner executes runner_execution with the repo-common scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper.

If starting outside the view, use the repo-common scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper or equivalent ADE setup to execute commands in the confirmed view environment; do not use the deployed ADE Lens RunInView.sh for this runner workflow.

For pre-cleanview ADE lifecycle checks in a PMC workflow, including ade pwv, ade lsco, ade showlabels, ade refreshview, and ade cleanview, keep the wrapper default FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC=TRUE path; do not run a standalone --full-fmwc entry before refreshview.

Use a minimum timeout budget of 600 seconds for every ADE command wrapper invocation, including read-only ADE checks, refreshview, cleanview, bug validation, checkin/save, OraReview, and MergeReq-related ADE preflight commands. Do not wrap ADE commands with timeout 120; refreshview commonly needs up to 10 minutes, and PMC or SeedData-specific helpers may use longer budgets when documented.

For actual PMC setup/run commands through scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh, pass --full-fmwc in the ADE session that will run pmc_wrapper so FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC is not set; regular non-PMC ADE tasks should keep the wrapper default FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC=TRUE path.

If ade cleanview is part of the PMC setup, run any needed refreshview before cleanview, then after cleanview completes, exit out of that ADE view session and do not run any further commands in it; re-enter the same ADE view in a fresh --full-fmwc session before ade pwv, ade lsco, ade expand, or pmc_wrapper. That re-entered session is the PMC-intended ADE session.

When using scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh, cleanview must be its own invocation; include an explicit blocking re-entry command such as scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh --full-fmwc -view <view> -- "cd $AVR; exit" only after cleanview. After that re-entry completes, batch sequential post-cleanview commands in that same fresh local wrapper session, using inline output checks for required checkpoints; do not re-enter the view for every command.
Never chain commands after cleanview in the same quoted command, shell, or ADE session.

When using scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh for the expand commands, keep $AVR inside the command argument passed to the local wrapper so it expands inside the ADE view session; do not let the outer /bin/bash -lc shell expand $AVR.
Run the expand commands before pmc_wrapper, exactly in the listed order.

After each expand command, inspect output inside the same loaded ADE session and stop before pmc_wrapper if it contains does not exist, skipping, Nothing to expand, or a resolved target such as /fusionapps/... or /fainteg that indicates $AVR was lost; do not split expand commands into separate RunInView.sh invocations solely to inspect output.

Before pmc_wrapper, check whether sqlplus works. If command -v sqlplus fails or sqlplus cannot load the Oracle client, and /usr/local/packages/oracleserver_remote/19.0.0/bin/sqlplus plus lib exist, export ORACLE_HOME=/usr/local/packages/oracleserver_remote/19.0.0 and LD_LIBRARY_PATH=${ORACLE_HOME}/lib in the same loaded ADE shell before pmc_wrapper; prepend ${ORACLE_HOME}/bin to PATH if needed.
After pmc_wrapper finishes, verify the generated PMC HTML and premerge artifacts before reporting success.
Treat PMC_CODE_ISSUE, PMC_CODE_ERROR, missing fusionapps/premerge/premerge_result.json, or a Central PMC section that contains only startup/initial checks as blocking.

If the PMC report shows BUG Validation Status: INVALID and no other PMC blocker, hand off manual HCM bug validation to the parent runner with CODEX_RUNNER_COMMAND: hcm.bug-validation bug_ref={{bug}}. Bug validation does not need an ADE view; require exit code 0 plus standalone PASS before continuing.

When manual bug validation PASSes, upload the parent stdout log or captured .txt output to BugDB with $CODEX_HOME/skills/dev-bugdb-helper/scripts/run-bugdb.sh attachment-upload {{bug}} <txt-file>, verify the attachment, and proceed; if manual bug validation is non-PASS, stop with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
If any blocking PMC marker is present, do not report PMC success and do not proceed to mergereq; include CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input with the exact report path and blocker.
Stop on the first failing command, report the command, exit code, and useful error text, and include CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
command_sequence: if ! command -v sqlplus >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -x /usr/local/packages/oracleserver_remote/19.0.0/bin/sqlplus ]; then export ORACLE_HOME=/usr/local/packages/oracleserver_remote/19.0.0; export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=${ORACLE_HOME}/lib; export PATH=${ORACLE_HOME}/bin:${PATH}; fi

commands.label: Run HCM PMC outside Codex

parameters.name: view
parameters.label: ADE view
parameters.description: Confirmed ADE view whose active transaction is rnerolu_bug-{{bug}}.

commands.label: Submit Merge Request
commands.template: mergereq submit view={{view}} bug={{bug}} mode={{mode}} manager={{manager}} release={{release}} cc={{cc}}

examples: refreshview to latest label and submit mergereq
parameter_hints: Use the current issue or resumed session context to infer the ADE view, active transaction, and bug when the follow-up does not repeat them.
Default missing mode to normal, default missing manager to alok.x.sahay@oracle.com, omit release when not supplied, and omit CC when not supplied.
If the requester asks to refreshview to latest label and submit MergeReq, include refreshview before submit in the proposed sequence.

If the same issue or resumed session already verified clean PMC/premerge evidence and uploaded PASS HCM bug-validation evidence for the unchanged transaction, do not include a separate repeat-evidence verification step.

instructions: Use the mergereq skill for submission workflow, property checks, and command generation.
Use Mergereq MCP tools for report-card details and current-transaction quality previews before submitting when available.

For HCM/ADE MergeReq submit or resend, default missing mode to normal email submit, default missing manager to alok.x.sahay@oracle.com, omit release unless explicitly provided or required by mergereq, and omit CC unless explicitly provided. Do not ask for these values when they are omitted.
For mode=normal, generate the normal email-submit MergeReq command, not check-only and not db-only/no-mail.

Before submitting or resending mergereq for HCM/ADE work, verify the latest PMC evidence for the active transaction unless the same issue or resumed session already verified clean PMC/premerge evidence and uploaded PASS HCM bug-validation evidence for this unchanged transaction.

When that evidence is already verified and the transaction has not changed, do not add a separate repeat-evidence verification step before refreshview or MergeReq submit; perform only minimal read-only ADE state checks needed to execute the approved sequence. When only checking existing PMC evidence before MergeReq, use lightweight read-only/ADE lifecycle checks; do not perform a full-FMWC re-entry unless rerunning PMC or entering the post-cleanview PMC setup session is actually required.
If the requester asks to refreshview to the latest label and submit MergeReq, include refreshview before submit after the required approval is present.

Do not submit mergereq when the latest PMC report has PMC_CODE_ISSUE, PMC_CODE_ERROR, missing premerge_result.json, or otherwise incomplete Central PMC results. If the only PMC concern is BUG Validation Status: INVALID, require manual HCM bug validation to PASS and upload the txt evidence to the bug first; report unresolved blockers with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input instead.

commands.label: Merge Approved ADE Transaction

examples: MergeReq is approved, proceed with merge
merge approved transaction after mergereq approval
approved mergereq can you proceed with merge
parameter_hints: Use the current issue or resumed session context to infer the ADE view and active transaction when the follow-up does not repeat them.
If the approved/move-ready MergeReq report card or target view cannot be verified, ask for the missing value with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input.
Refresh the view before merge only when the requester explicitly allowed it and ADE state indicates refresh is needed.

instructions: This intent is for post-approval merge only, not PMC and not MergeReq submit/resend.

Approved MergeReq merge: treat this as a post-approval ADE merge workflow. Verify the approved/move-ready report card and active view/transaction, refresh the view only if needed and allowed, then use `ade savetrans; ade beginmerge; ade mergetrans; ade endmerge`.
Do not rerun PMC and do not submit, resend, or reroute MergeReq unless explicitly requested.
Run merge commands from the confirmed ADE view and stop on the first ADE error.

commands.label: CodeSearch
commands.template: codesearch search query={{query}} full={{full}} definition={{definition}} symbol={{symbol}} path={{path}} type={{type}} ade_label={{ade_label}} ade_label_series={{ade_label_series}}
instructions: Use the CodeSearch MCP search tool for ADE/OpenGrok searches and summarize contextual snippets.
Provide ade_label or ade_label_series when not running inside the target ADE view.
Map query to the most appropriate CodeSearch field, usually full unless symbol or definition is more specific.

runner_execution.summary: Collect a bounded, read-only project snapshot outside the LLM session.
